levels.desc: Variables, loops, functions, Pandas. Ex: Scripting daily stock price downloads.
OOP, API integration, Git/GitHub. Ex: Pulling live exchange rates from Bloomberg API.
Code optimization, CI/CD, distributed computing. Ex: Standardizing a bank's Python codebase.

whyWeLearn: The foundational programming language for modern data and AI work.

levels.desc: SELECT, WHERE, JOIN, GROUP BY. Ex: Finding all customers who deposited >$10k this week.
Window Functions, Subqueries, CTEs. Ex: Calculating rolling 30-day average balances.
Query optimization, indexing. Ex: Tuning a query that scans 50 million transaction rows.

whyWeLearn: The ability to store, extract, and transform massive amounts of financial records.

topic: Core Foundation: Domain Knowledge & Soft Skills

levels.desc: Basic banking terms (ROI, Churn, AML), asking clarifying questions.
Data storytelling, regulatory awareness (GDPR, Basel III), stakeholder management.
Cross-departmental strategy, driving a data-first culture, C-Suite communication.

whyWeLearn: Data is completely useless if it doesn't solve a real business problem.

levels.desc: Excel (Pivots), Tableau/PowerBI basics. Ex: Visualizing monthly loan application volumes.
Hypothesis testing (A/B), anomaly detection. Ex: A/B testing a new mobile banking layout.
Causal inference, Monte Carlo. Ex: Stress-testing capital reserves under market crash scenarios.

whyWeLearn: The ability to explore, clean, and visualize financial data to answer 'what happened?'

levels.desc: Linear/Logistic Regression. Ex: Predicting binary loan defaults (Yes/No).
Random Forest, XGBoost, SMOTE. Ex: Detecting credit card fraud in highly imbalanced datasets.
Deep Learning, Reinforcement Learning, SHAP. Ex: LSTMs for algorithmic high-frequency trading.

whyWeLearn: The ability to use historical data and math to answer 'what will happen next?'

levels.desc: Prompt engineering, coding assistants. Ex: Summarizing a 50-page central bank policy PDF.
RAG, building internal chatbots. Ex: Chatbot that safely answers questions based on private financials.
Fine-tuning open-source models, multi-agent systems. Ex: Auditing employee emails for insider trading.

whyWeLearn: Using Generative AI to automate text analysis, accelerate coding, and improve decision-making.

levels.desc: Primary/Foreign keys, structured vs unstructured. Ex: Designing a simple schema for user profiles.
Cloud Data Warehouses (BigQuery, Snowflake). Ex: Designing an enterprise data warehouse.
Data Lakehouse (Databricks), distributed systems. Ex: Architecting storage for 10 yrs of stock ticks.

whyWeLearn: Understanding how to securely and efficiently structure massive datasets.

levels.desc: Python scripts for ETL. Ex: Moving daily CSV reports from an SFTP server into SQL.
dbt, Apache Airflow. Ex: Building a reliable daily pipeline that calculates credit risk scores.
Infrastructure as Code, monitoring. Ex: Managing 500+ pipelines for a bank's entire reporting suite.

whyWeLearn: Automating the flow of data from messy raw sources into clean, usable tables.

levels.desc: Batch vs Streaming concepts. Ex: Understanding nightly updates vs real-time processing.
Event-driven architecture, RabbitMQ. Ex: Pushing an alert the second a large withdrawal happens.
Kafka, Spark, Flink. Ex: Real-time ingestion engine for thousands of credit card swipes per second.

whyWeLearn: Handling data that moves too fast or is too large for traditional databases.

levels.desc: Basic PII masking and data privacy concepts.
RBAC, Docker/FastAPI deployments. Ex: Serving a Data Scientist's fraud model as a REST API.
CI/CD for data, compliance. Ex: Ensuring automated GDPR data deletion cascades across all tables.

whyWeLearn: Ensuring data is secure, AI models are reliable, and pipelines comply with strict financial regulations.
